name: rivea corymbosa
safetyNotes: May cause moderate risk: side effects or medication interactions are plausible. avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician, do not combine with serotonergic drugs or maois without medical guidance, nausea, vasoconstriction, dizziness [0, 4], may interact with maois or may interact with ssris. Avoid with psychiatric conditions; Avoid with cardiovascular disease; Avoid in pregnancy; Avoid with 4]; Avoid with heart conditions.
description: Rivea corymbosa is a medicinal herb traditionally used for visual hallucinations and euphoria.
dosage: 20, 100 seeds traditionally ground into cold water infusion.
legalStatus: Regulated in some countries; seeds often legal
traditionalUse: Sacred plant for healing, prophecy, and communion with spirits.
